name: Roast Pork with Spiced Cherry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Rub the pork roast with salt, pepper, and sage.
2. Place the roast in an ungreased shallow baking pan.
3. Bake at 325°F for 1½ to 2 hours, or until a meat thermometer reads 160-170°F.
4. Meanwhile, drain the cherries and set them aside.
5. Reserve the cherry liquid and add water to measure ¾ cup.
6. Pour half of the liquid into a saucepan with sugar and vinegar.
7. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes.
8. Remove and discard the spices.
9. In a small bowl, combine cornstarch with the remaining cherry liquid until smooth.
10. Add this mixture back to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
11. Cook for 2 more minutes, stirring constantly.
12. Stir in lemon juice, butter, cherries, and food coloring (if desired).
13. Let the roast stand for 10 minutes before slicing.
14. Serve with the cherry sauce.
